steps.dialogue: Improper fractions can seem strange at first, particularly because when we started we talked about fractions only up to one whole. Now we're learning that fractions - improper fractions - can be bigger than that. The key is keeping those equal jumps the same size no matter where you are on the number line. Even when you go past 1 whole, the jumps stay equal. Let's do a little practice to start.
Great work! You can place fractions on a number line, even beyond 1. You understand that the spacing stays the same no matter where you are.
